title: teucrium chamaedrys subsp. tauricola
intro: Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. tauricola, a subspecies of the perennial herb Teucrium chamaedrys, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the Dinaric Alps in the Balkans. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high content of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. tauricola include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
